stance: Risk-on, pro-cyclical: overweight metals/energy and power-capex where momentum AND analyst revisions both lead, anchored by a cheap large-cap financials core; underweight weak-revision power/cement laggards and expensive defensives.
experience_note: Pre-registered watch items (anti-hindsight): (1) The book lives or dies on the commodity/capex up-cycle — I am watching LME base-metal and Brent trends plus the order-book/capex commentary in power-T&D names; if metals momentum rolls over while analyst revisions in VEDL/NMDC/NATIONALUM start falling, the overweight is wrong and I cut it. (2) The revision-led tech sleeve (COFORGE/TCS) is a bet that lagging price mean-reverts toward improving estimates; if price keeps falling AND revisions stop rising over the next quarter, the thesis failed and I exit rather than average down. (3) I am explicitly NOT betting on rate-sensitive defensives or expensive consumer — if leadership rotates to FMCG/quality-defensives on a growth scare, this book underperforms and I would rotate. What would change my mind broadly: a clear risk-off regime (NIFTY 100 breadth collapse, FII outflows) would make my 1.09-beta cyclical tilt a liability and I would de-risk toward the financial/defensive core.
